---
title: "PubPeer Comments (ref_pubpeer)"
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{PubPeer Comments}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---



## What it checks

The `ref_pubpeer` module cross-references the paper's **cited references** against [PubPeer](https://pubpeer.com/), a platform for post-publication peer review. If a paper you cite has comments on PubPeer, that may be worth knowing â€” comments can range from minor clarifications to serious concerns about the work.

The module reports which cited references have PubPeer comments, how many, and a link, so you can read the discussion and judge its relevance yourself.

::: callout-note
This module makes **live network calls** to PubPeer (matching references by DOI). You need an internet connection to run the code below.
:::

## Running the module

`demopaper()` cites a reference that has PubPeer comments.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
paper <- demopaper()
mo <- module_run(paper, "ref_pubpeer")
mo$traffic_light
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
#> [1] "info"
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
mo$summary_text
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
#> [1] "You cited 1 reference with comments in PubPeer."
```


:::
:::


The `table` lists each matched reference, the number of comments, and a link:


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
mo$table[, c("doi", "total_comments", "url")] |>
  knitr::kable()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}


|doi                      | total_comments|url                                                             |
|:------------------------|--------------:|:---------------------------------------------------------------|
|10.1177/0956797614520714 |              3|https://pubpeer.com/publications/3FA648ECECB88454C91804F09E2E56 |


:::
:::


## Running on many papers


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
mo <- module_run(psychsci[1:10], "ref_pubpeer")
mo$summary_table
```
:::


## Interpreting the result

A `green`/empty result means none of the cited references had PubPeer comments. A flagged result does **not** mean a citation is bad â€” it means there is a discussion you may want to read. Follow the `url` to the PubPeer thread and judge the comments in context.

## Options

`ref_pubpeer` takes only the `paper` argument.

## Notes

In the Shiny app, querying PubPeer is an opt-in checkbox, because it sends the cited DOIs to an external service.
